title: How to safely inject the tear trough & remove dark circles.
filename: How to safely inject the tear trough & remove dark circles..en.vtt
excerpt: Good idea generally if you're anywh near the tear trough is to have a feel that you're on top of bone before you start injecting every now and then I've trained someone who's got their bearings wrong and they end up pointing towards the orbit before we start so always have a good…

segments.text: good idea generally if you're anywh near the tear trough is to have a feel that you're on top of bone before you start injecting every now and then I've trained someone who's got their bearings wrong and they end up pointing towards the orbit before we start so always have a good feel of the shape of the bone and you're imagining underneath that if I go in I should be pretty close to the apex of the zygoma so see how superficial that is I didn't have to go deep if you're plunging too deep you've probably gone either into the cheek or if you're really off bound you might have gone into the orbit so don't inject until you feel stable surface like [Music] this so we're now going to do a little bit of canula work in the tear trough I like using canula when the volume is

very low I don't need to put a lot in here it's not a deep Groove I just want to elevate this tissue just a little bit and I want to decrease the chance of bruising cuz we have a little bruise here already so we'll do a pilot hole that's angled up so I can reach here I'm always thinking what's the where will I want to be the whole time cuz that dictates my entry point which should which it should be on this line here I also want to be superior to the zymatic ligament is probably over here so I want to be Superior to that and then slide in underneath the orbicular oculi into the sou and gently fill it just into the hypodermic fat quite rough entries in with canula if you just take your time and feel with the tip like this you can slide in and once you're in the hypodermic fats then you just find the easy path which is usually there remembering the angle I made the initial hole

initial hole there we go now I'm in now I can edge up patiently finding my way no need to push too hard there's always somehow always find a way there we go and now into the sou can feel that the canula tip I can see by the movement that it's now into the place where I want to be I'm using movement is the main guide the Ripple in the skin is a great guide where the tip of the kandar is and I've gone all the way up to the top there I don't think there's any need to really aspirate here if you've been gentle but I do it out of habit and now I start to trickle in fact I'm going to rotate this around so that the can is facing down a little bit deeper and I'm going to start to trickle in product look how slowly this bevel is moving it's almost imperceptible to be honest very slow small amounts of product painting it

[Music] I remembered from the consultation what she said about a little Shadow here and I can actually do this through the same entry point I just have to cross over and head towards the mid cheek and I don't think this will completely get rid of it but it will help so there is a muscle insertion here that's pulling on this area when you smile this should soften it slightly you need a product that's very forgiving in this area really only two I would consider toxan redensity 2 and Bella so recap on the entry point I'm aiming for the sou just inferior to the orbit my entry point is dictated by that and where I suspect the bulk of orbicular oculi is so I will enter about here don't need to make a track all the way into the tear trough just need to get into the hypodermis that's as deep as you need to go [Music]

[Music] so gentle probe looking for an easy path this is a 27 gauge canul so it is quite cutting if you're rough but if you're not rough it's without doubt less likely to bruise and canate a vessel than a needle I know there's a catchphrase of saying it's just as cutting as a needle that's factually not true it's many times thicker at its tip than a needle tip so if you're gentle it's [Music] better there we go patience has paid off once again slid into the right spot I can see the tip of the canyon now all the way up where I need to go and then we can start painting the product on very small amounts at a time

I can't really see it the plunger moving but I can see the filler itself appearing and that's what I'm using as a guide to how much I'm putting in I'm not actually watching the plunger at all but I'm putting about the lowest amount of force possible that I can control very low pressure injection I find it quite useful with tear truff to use the broad base of my thumb to just do a little bit of shaping massage and then the Q-tips very useful for more precise adjustments there a little flat spot that's caught my tension just there and maybe a little bit there so I'm just going to this would be better with a needle on bone so we'll make another little adjustment [Music]
